steps.title: Find the perimeter of square Q
steps.content:
Square P has side length $x$, so its perimeter is $4x$.

Square Q's perimeter is 32 inches greater than square P's perimeter:
$$\text{Perimeter of Q} = 4x + 32$$

steps.title: Determine the side length of square Q
steps.content:
The side length of square Q equals its perimeter divided by 4:
$$\text{Side of Q} = \frac{4x + 32}{4} = \frac{4(x + 8)}{4} = x + 8$$

steps.title: Express the area function
steps.content:
The area of square Q is the square of its side length:
$$f(x) = (x + 8)^2$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
